situational_context: Week 1 of structured return-to-running after 182-day gap (last run 2025-11-02). First comeback run completed 2026-05-03: 2.47 km, 21 min, Z2, suffer 7 — conservative and appropriate. Body weight 104.8 kg (+20 kg above running-peak era) creates elevated tissue-tolerance risk per return-to-running protocol. Cycling base maintained via ebike commuting and occasional gravel. ACWR normalized at 1.06 after functional overreaching spike from May 1 gravel ride (suffer 55). HRV stable at 38 (BALANCED), RHR improved to 49 bpm. Training readiness 75 today. Vitamin D remains insufficient (27 ng/mL, Dec 2025). VO2max running recalibrated to 38.6 after first run data. Weight trending down (106.9→104.8 kg over 3 weeks, ~0.7 kg/wk — within safe range for tendon adaptation). Planning now governed by return-to-running protocol: max 2 runs/week, Z1-Z2 only, surface preference soft, tissue feedback required before each run.

plan_7d_ahead.type: Z2 Cycling or short Z1-Z2 Run

recommendations.text: Follow return-to-running protocol strictly: no more than 1 additional short run this week (Saturday May 9), max 2.5 km, Z1-Z2 only, soft surface (Bois de Vincennes), walk breaks if needed. Report any localized pain (Achilles, patellar, tibia) lasting >24h — that aborts next run.
recommendations.measurable: Saturday run ≤2.5 km at avg HR ≤140; zero pain reports >24h post-run

recommendations.text: Cap cycling load on sur site commute days: prefer ebike over gravel this week to keep ACWR within [0.8-1.3] given the running introduction. If gravel taken one day, next day should be ebike or rest.
recommendations.measurable: Weekly ACWR remains ≤1.3; no single commute day suffer score >40

recommendations.text: Maintain protein intake toward 150g target — last 7d averaged 128g. Prioritize post-session protein (20-30g within 2h) to support tendon collagen synthesis during the running rebuild.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Any localized pain >24h after May 3 or May 9 run
markers_to_watch.reason: Return-to-running protocol: 104.8 kg at +20 kg above running era weight means 25% higher impact forces. Achilles and patellar tendon are primary risk sites.
Running load being added on top of existing cycling base. Gravel commutes could spike ACWR if not managed.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Watch for drop >10% from current 38 (i.e. below 34) for 3+ consecutive days
markers_to_watch.reason: Would indicate recovery capacity not keeping up with combined running + cycling load introduction.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Target gradual increase toward 35-40 over next 2 weeks
markers_to_watch.reason: Currently at 1.47% of historical peak. Steady growth confirms rebuild trajectory without overreaching.
